seasonArc: 2026 was Jakob Söderqvist's first full WorldTour season, the 22-year-old Swede stepping up from Lidl-Trek's development squad to the WorldTeam as a time-trial specialist and all-rounder rather than a pure climber. The early months were a learning curve — completing stage races like the AlUla Tour and the Boucles de la Mayenne without headline results, and a prologue second place in April hinting at the engine. His breakthrough came at the Tour de Hongrie in May, where he transformed his season in a single afternoon: on the rain-shortened queen stage to Pécs he attacked clear on the climbing circuit to win the stage and seize the overall lead, then defended it on the Veszprém finale to take the overall by 40 seconds from Benoît Cosnefroy. It was his first stage-race overall victory and the kind of result that marks a neo-pro out as one to watch, confirming the all-round potential Lidl-Trek had promoted him for.
